title: One-Liner Arsenal
learning_objectives: Build reusable Bash one-liners for common offensive tasks
key_topics: Text processing (grep, awk, cut, sort, uniq)
Edge case handling in CLI operations
scenario: Your team does the same recon steps on every engagement. It is time to automate. Build an arsenal of 6 working command-line one-liners that can be reused on future engagements.

scenario_context.relevance: Efficient operators automate repetitive tasks. Having a tested arsenal of one-liners saves time during time-sensitive engagements.

subtasks.title: Build 6 One-Liners
subtasks.description: Create one-liners for: extract unique IPs from a log, find SUID binaries, list listening ports, extract emails from text, count error lines in logs, find recently modified files.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Each one-liner should be a single pipe chain. Use grep, awk, cut, sort, uniq, find, netstat/ss as appropriate. Test each against real data.
subtasks.evidence_required: All 6 one-liners with working output screenshots

subtasks.title: Document Each
subtasks.description: For each one-liner, explain what it does, how each pipe stage works, and when you would use it in a pentest.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Break down each pipe stage. Example: 'cat access.log' reads the file, '| grep -oE "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+"' extracts IPs, '| sort | uniq -c | sort -rn' counts and ranks them.

subtasks.title: Stress Test
subtasks.description: Run each against sample data. Verify they work and handle edge cases (empty files, special characters).
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Test each with: normal data, empty files, files with special characters, large files. Document any failures and how you fixed them.

reflection_prompt: You're on an engagement and need to quickly extract all IP addresses that connected to a compromised web server. Write the command and explain why speed matters in incident response.

quiz.question: The 'sort | uniq -c | sort -rn' pattern is used to:
options: Count occurrences of each unique line and rank by frequency
quiz.explanation: This common pattern: sorts for grouping, counts unique occurrences, then sorts numerically in reverse to show most frequent first.

quiz.question: Testing one-liners with empty files is important because:
options: Empty files are common on servers
Commands may produce errors or unexpected output with no input
quiz.explanation: Many commands behave differently or produce errors when given no input. Robust one-liners should handle edge cases gracefully.

mentor_notes: Each one-liner must actually work — no theoretical commands. The documentation quality shows understanding. Edge case handling separates good from great submissions.
